title: US Gold Standard
significance: The Coinage Act of 1792 establishes gold at $19.39/oz as the basis of US currency
driver: Legal foundation of gold-backed money

title: Gold Standard Act
significance: The Gold Standard Act officially places the US on the gold standard at $20.67/oz
driver: US government formally backs currency with gold

title: Gold Confiscation
significance: Executive Order 6102 makes private gold ownership illegal in the US
driver: Great Depression — government seizes private gold

title: Gold Reserve Act
significance: Roosevelt revalues gold from $20.67 to $35/oz — a 69% devaluation of the dollar
driver: Dollar debasement to combat deflation

title: Bretton Woods
significance: Bretton Woods Agreement establishes gold at $35/oz as anchor for global monetary system
driver: Post-WWII global financial order

title: London Gold Pool Dissolution
significance: The London Gold Pool collapses after central banks fail to suppress the gold price
driver: Central bank cooperation breaks down under gold demand pressure

title: Nixon Shock
significance: Nixon ends dollar convertibility to gold — Bretton Woods collapses
driver: US suspends gold standard — fiat era begins

title: Gold Futures Trading Begins
significance: Gold futures begin trading on COMEX, opening gold to leveraged speculation
driver: Futures markets create new price discovery mechanism

title: Private Ownership Restored
significance: US citizens can legally own gold again after 42 years
driver: Gold legalization drives retail demand

title: First Major Peak
significance: Gold reaches $850/oz driven by inflation, oil crisis, and Soviet invasion of Afghanistan
driver: Stagflation + geopolitical crisis + Hunt brothers silver speculation

title: 20-Year Low
significance: Gold hits a 20-year low at $252/oz — central banks selling reserves
driver: Strong dollar, low inflation, central bank sales

title: Washington Agreement on Gold
significance: Central banks agree to limit gold sales — price jumps 25% in days
driver: Coordinated central bank policy to stabilize gold

title: GLD ETF Launch
significance: SPDR Gold Trust (GLD) launches — first US gold ETF democratizes gold investing
driver: Gold becomes accessible to retail investors via stock market

title: First $1,000
significance: Gold crosses $1,000/oz for the first time amid Bear Stearns collapse and financial crisis
driver: Financial crisis safe-haven demand

title: Post-GFC Peak
significance: Gold reaches $1,920/oz — peak of post-2008 crisis bull run
driver: QE, European debt crisis, US credit downgrade

title: Flash Crash
significance: Gold crashes $200 in two days — largest drop in 30 years
driver: Fed taper talk, forced ETF liquidations, margin calls

title: Bear Market Bottom
significance: Gold bottoms at $1,046 — down 45% from 2011 peak
driver: Strong dollar, rising rates, deflationary fears

title: Breaks $1,400
significance: Gold breaks above $1,400 for first time since 2013 on dovish Fed pivot
driver: Fed signals rate cuts — dollar weakens

title: New All-Time High
significance: Gold sets new ATH at $2,075 amid pandemic stimulus and zero rates
driver: Massive QE, negative real rates, pandemic uncertainty

title: Central Banks Record Buying
significance: Central banks purchase record 1,037 tonnes in 2023 — second year of massive accumulation
driver: De-dollarization, geopolitical hedging, reserve diversification

title: Breaks $2,100
significance: Gold breaks decisively above $2,100 resistance — new price discovery begins
driver: Central bank buying + rate cut expectations

title: Breaks $2,500
significance: Gold crosses $2,500 amid massive central bank accumulation and geopolitical tensions
driver: De-dollarization trend, BRICS demand, Middle East conflict

title: Gold Surges Past $3,000
significance: Gold breaks the historic $3,000 barrier for the first time
driver: Persistent inflation fears, central bank buying, geopolitical uncertainty

title: Gold Hits $5,595 ATH
significance: Gold reaches all-time high of $5,595/oz — unprecedented surge
driver: Record central bank accumulation, fiscal deficit concerns, global de-dollarization
